_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use strategies like predicting, connecting and asking questions to help us understand stories and texts we hear, see or read.

_source.w: We are learning to use strategies like predicting, connecting and asking questions to help us understand stories and texts we hear, see or read.
y: Comprehension strategies help us make sense of what we read and hear.
When we predict and question, we are actively thinking — not just passively listening.
These strategies help us understand even when we come across unfamiliar words or ideas.
eg: We look at the cover of a book and predict what the story might be about.
We answer questions about a familiar story by putting events in order.
We connect something in a story to something that happened in our own life.
We stop during a story to ask 'What will happen next?'
We retell a well-known story in order using picture prompts.
We make a prediction before turning the page and check if we were right.
